text: She is only wearing a laryngeal mask.
The incision is made just below the syphoid process of the sternum and the linea alba is incised.
The sci-foot process is then identified and the surgeon pushes his finger under the sternum for blunt dissection.
Local anesthetic solution is administered.
Then a skyhook is positioned in order to pull the sternum upwards and produce more space for the surgery to be performed in the mediastinum.
Theracoscopic instruments are introduced to proceed with the dissection under the sternum.
The surgeon makes his way to the right pleural space.
We are now in the right pleural cavity.
The right mediastinal pleura is incised off the pericardium using the ligature, getting as close as possible to the right phrenic nerve to reduce the amount of fatty tissue that is left behind.
A second line of incision is made on the mediastinal pleura behind the sternum just medial to the right internal mammary artery and it is joint with the first line of incision.
The dissection of the thymus off the back of the sternum is carried on using the ligature and retraction from a metal sucker.
The thymus is now being dissected from the medial border of the SVC and then, going up to the nominate vein, dissection of the right superior horn of the thymus takes place.
The thymus is now being dissected from the anterior face of the pericardium and of the SVC.
The dissection continues, the left innominate vein is found and thymic veins leading to it are divided by ligature.
Dissection is now continued up over the anointed artery and then towards the trachea.
The right phrenic nerve is injected with local anesthetic for pain relief and in order to reduce movements of the right diaphragm to facilitate the dissection.
Once dissection has been completed on the right side, standard diathermy is used to get into the left pleural space.
At this point, a small amount of positive pressure is administered in our areas through a laryngeal mask.
then dissection of the back of the sternum is continued upwards using the left internal mammary artery as a landmark.
A second plane of dissection is carried on towards the left phrenic nerve and then, following the course of the nerve, keeping a safe margin from it, the two planes of dissection are joined together.
The thymus is being dissected off the anterior face of the pericardium, keeping as much fatty tissue into the sample as possible.
The gussure is used to divide the branches from the left internal mammary artery and vein leading to the thymus.
The thymus blood supply are small vessels from the internal mammary artery, from the inferior thyroid arteries as well as pericardiofrenic branches.
We're now up into the neck looking for the thyrothymic ligament which is the most superior margin of the horn.
The left superior horn is being dissected from the left denominate vein.

ligature is an excellent energy device for thymectomy as it can divide all the small vessels and fatty tissue with a very bloodless plane and it has a very little thermal energy spread which is good when going near the phrenix and it also reduces any potential damage to the innominate vein the last part of thymic tissue is separated from the left innominate vein
The dissection of the thymus at the spine is almost complete.
The thymus has now been excised and taken out of the patient's chest.
We can now appreciate the amazing view on both the pleural spaces and the anterior mediastinum achievable with a subcyphoid approach.

We can see the two lungs on the sides, the pericardium in the middle and above it we can see the left innominate vein which is now being checked for any residual tissue and below that the aortic arch with the brachycephalic artery visible behind the innominate vein and further that the trachea.
We can also appreciate the movements of the chest wall indicating that the patient is spontaneously breathing.
Then the skin closure begins and the drain tube is positioned and linked to a digital chest drainage with suction on.
As the drainage showed no air leak the tube was removed straight away.
Lastly the skin is closed and anesthetic solution is administered on the wound.
Here you can see the final length of the surgical wound which is about 4 cm.
